title: Chapter 14: Battle Partners
content: The combat milestone was called "First Blood Together."

**[COMBAT MILESTONE: First Blood Together]**
**[Objective: Engage at least 5 zombies in combat alongside Seraphina Vale.]**
**[Condition: Marcus must personally dispatch at least 1 zombie.]**
**[Bonus: For each zombie Marcus kills, Sera's Affection +2 (max +10)]**
**[Reward: Perimeter Defense Turrets + Sera Affection +5 + Party Combat Synergy Bonus]**

Marcus had never killed anything in his life. He'd never even been in a real fight—the closest he'd come was a shoving match in seventh grade that had ended with both boys crying and the teacher asking if they needed the counselor.
Now he needed to kill a zombie. With a knife.

The System had provided one—a tactical blade that sat in the hidden sheath of his Survivor's Edge jacket, balanced for throwing or close combat. He'd held it for ten minutes that morning, getting the weight of it, the cold of the steel, the way the edge caught the light like a whisper of intent.
"You're overthinking it," Sera said from the doorway.
"I'm *preparing*."

"You're staring at a knife like it's going to grade your performance." She crossed the room in three steps—still too fast for his eyes to track comfortably—and stood in front of him. "Killing isn't complicated. It's the decision that's hard. You decide to live. Then you live. Everything else is logistics."
"Easy for you to say. You're a zombie queen with super-strength and supernatural speed. I'm a guy who peaked in Gold rank on Overwatch."
"Gold is respectable."
"It's the median, Sera."

"Then be median. Be exactly as capable as an average human who has decided, fully and completely, that he is not going to die today." She took the knife from his hand, examined it, pressed the hilt back into his palm. "This blade will go through a zombie's skull if you put your weight behind it. Aim for the eye socket or the temple—thinnest bone, least resistance. Don't aim for the forehead; you'll just get your knife stuck."
"How do you know this?"

"I've killed forty-seven zombies since I woke up. I'm very good at it, and I'm going to teach you to be adequate." The faintest smile. "Now. There are Howlers in the building's lower floors. Eight of them, wandered in through the basement access. We're going to clear them out."
"*Eight?*"
"Eight is practice. Two hundred is the exam." She headed for the door. "Come on. I'll be right beside you. And Marcus?"
"Yeah?"
"When you kill your first one, don't throw up on it. It's undignified."
The lower floors of Oakwood Heights had become a charnel house.

Not from the current siege—from weeks of neglect, of doors left open, of residents who'd fled or died or turned without anyone left to clean up. Marcus moved through the second-floor hallway with his knife drawn, stepping over debris and trying to breathe through his mouth because the smell was a living thing, a physical presence that clawed at his sinuses.
Sera moved beside him—not in front, not behind. *Beside*. Equal positioning, combat formation, the way you'd walk with someone you trusted to cover your flank.
"Three ahead," she murmured. "Around the corner. Howlers—they know we're here."
"How?"

"Vibration. They feel footsteps through the floor." She pressed her back to the wall, peered around the corner. "Two males, one female. Male left is missing an arm—less dangerous. Female center is the scout—she'll signal the others. Male right is the tank—big, slow, ignore him until the others are down."
"Order?"

"I take the tank. You take the scout. The one-armed one, whoever gets to it first." She looked at him. "When I move, you move. No hesitation. No second-guessing. Trust theplan, trust me, trust yourself."
"Trust myself. The guy who's killed zero zombies."
"Today you kill one. That's how records start."
She moved.

The hallway became a blur. Sera hit the tank like a comet—fist-sized impact to its chest, staggering it, then a spinning kick that took its legs out from under it. The Howler went down, and Sera was on top of it, fists hammering.
The scout Howler bolted toward Marcus.

Time didn't slow—nothing so cinematic. But his senses sharpened, the way they did in a ranked match when the clutch was on the line, when one mistake meant elimination and every micro-decision mattered.
The Howler was fast. Fast and close and its mouth was open and its teeth were—

Marcus stepped left. Not back. *Left*. Angling off the creature's charge vector, exactly like kiting a melee boss, exactly like the thousand ranked matches where positioning was the difference between a win screen and a gray one.
The Howler's momentum carried it past him. His knife came up—not thinking, not aiming, just *moving*—and the blade caught the back of its skull where the spine met the occipital bone.
The Howler dropped.
His knife was stuck. He yanked—it came free with a sound like pulling a boot from mud—and the Howler didn't get up.

**[ZOMBIE KILL: Marcus Chen — Howler Scout]**
**[First Kill!]**
**[Sera Affection: 60 → 62/100]**
The one-armed Howler lunged at him from behind. Marcus spun, knife raised—
Sera's hand caught it mid-air. She twisted, and something in the Howler's neck cracked, and it went limp.
"Nice kill," she said, dropping the body. "The step-left was good. Gamer instincts."
"My whole life was training for this moment and I didn't even know it."

The remaining five Howlers were on the first floor. Marcus and Sera cleared them in four minutes—Sera handling three simultaneously while Marcus covered the stairwell, catching one that tried to flank with a desperate, graceless slash that opened its throat and dropped it.
His second kill was messier than his first. His third was faster. By the fourth, his hands had stopped shaking.

**[COMBAT MILESTONE: First Blood Together — COMPLETE]**
**[Zombies eliminated: 8]**
**[Marcus kills: 3]**
**[Sera kills: 5]**
**[Combined synergy rating: B+ (effective teamwork, needs refinement)]**
**[Rewards:]**
**[- Perimeter Defense Turrets (4x installed at building corners)]**
**[- Sera Affection: 62 → 69/100]**
**[- Party Combat Synergy Bonus: Marcus + Sera coordinated attacks deal 20% more damage]**
**[- NEW ABILITY: Battle Link — Marcus can feel Sera's combat positioning instinctively]**

The Battle Link settled into his mind like a sixth sense—a faint pressure at the back of his skull that told him where Sera was without looking. Not words or images, just awareness. She was to his left. She was moving fast. She was safe.
"You feel it?" Sera asked.
"Yeah. It's like... GPS for you."
"It goes both ways. I can feel your heartbeat now." A pause. "It's slower than I expected. You're calmer than you should be."
"I just killed three zombies. I think my body's still figuring out the appropriate emotional response."
"Figure it out later. We have fortifications to install and a horde to stop."

**[FORTIFICATION PROGRESS: 4/4 objectives complete]**
**[All rewards unlocked!]**
**[- Door Reinforcement Pack: INSTALLED]**
**[- Perimeter Defense Turrets: INSTALLED]**
**[- Frequency Dampener: INSTALLED]**
**[- Emergency Bunker: INSTALLED]**
The safehouse was ready. Titanium walls, automated turrets, frequency dampening, and a last-resort bunker beneath the building.
And Marcus Chen—who'd never thrown a punch in his life—had killed three zombies without throwing up.
He was getting the hang of this apocalypse thing.
Sera touched his shoulder. Her fingers were still cold, but the touch was warmer than it had been a week ago.
"Six points to Affection 75," she said. "And approximately thirty hours until the horde arrives."
"How do we get six points?"
"Survive the next thirty hours together, and I imagine it'll happen organically." Her grey eyes held his. "Or you could kiss me. That's probably worth at least ten."
Marcus's brain short-circuited.
Sera was already walking away, and by the time he formulated a response, she was at the top of the stairs, Shadow Weave rippling, the ghost of a laugh trailing behind her like smoke.
The zombie queen was *flirting* with him.
And the horde was coming.
